provenance.derivedBy: inc-001, 2026-07-17. Derived from both manifests directly, not from PLAN.md 2.1's draft table. Counts: 28 exact, 13 rename, 3 a-only, 3 b-only — 44 on each side, 47 entries.
provenance.bIsSnapshot: The B side is a snapshot pinned to bSha. It becomes live-checkable at inc-005, when B's model is vendored under live-animals/model/ and mapping.test.js can import it. Until then the A side is the only side machine-checked against a live registry.

entries.note: System-minted at notification-creation time (GBN-AG-YY-XXXXXX). B declares it for V4 completeness and puts it on KNOWN_UNWIRED; A never models it because A's backend mints it and the mapper surfaces it. Not a capability gap.
Consumed from gov.identity on authentication; on B's KNOWN_UNWIRED allow-list. A carries no obligation for it. Not a capability gap.
Both unconditionally mandatory. A additionally carries enforcedAt: 'continue' — a flow-layer key with no B counterpart (see inc-018).
Both unconditionally mandatory yes/no. Pure rename.

DIVERGENCE beyond the rename — scope and purge disagree. A: activatedBy {equals 'yes'} + wipeOnExit, so 'no' takes it OUT of scope and DESTROYS the stored value. B: retain-value pattern — always inScope, mandatory on 'yes' / optional otherwise, and the comment cites V4 as saying the field is NOT purged on 'no'. The oracle will go red here. inc-002/inc-003 input.
Both unconditionally mandatory. Stored-value vocabulary differs: A's gate reads 'internalMarket' (camelCase), B's reads 'internal-market' (kebab code). PLAN 5.6, mechanical.
Same gate, same purge-on-flip, same mandate. Only the compared value differs ('internalMarket' vs 'internal-market').

Same shape (notification-level, any-line gate, mandatory-or-purged). Whitelist MEMBERSHIP differs: A's UNWEANED_ANIMAL_COMMODITIES is ['Cow','Horse']; B's is 5 codes (equines, cattle, pigs, sheep, goats). Not just a vocabulary difference — a different set of consignments gates in.

Same gate, same purge-on-flip, same mandate. Compared value differs: A 'Commercial', B 'commercial'. PLAN 5.6, mechanical.
Same gate, same purge-on-flip, same mandate. Compared value differs: A 'Private', B 'private'. PLAN 5.6, mechanical.
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.

DIVERGENCE beyond the name — same gate, different MANDATE. A: required: true, so once the land-transport gate opens the field is mandatory. B: status 'optional' in the whenTrue branch. Same name, opposite mandate. The oracle will go red here. inc-002 input. (Compared values also differ: A ['Railway','Road Vehicle'] vs B ['railway','road-vehicle'].)
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.
Unconditionally mandatory address block on both sides.
Optional on both sides (A by omitting required, B by status 'optional').
Unconditionally mandatory on both sides.

Plural-collection vs singular-record naming only. Both are structural, always-in-scope, user-driven groups with a floor of 1 (A requiredAtLeastOne, B requires.minEntries 1). Identity differs — A positional array index, B stable composite key — but that is inc-008's problem, not a mapping one.

Confirmed 1:1 — both live within the commodity-line group, both mandatory, and both are THE gate obligation for packages / cph / unweaned / every per-unit identifier. But the STORED VALUE differs in kind: A stores the display name ('Cow'), B stores the code ('0102'). B's name is the honest one; A's id is a UX name for the picker. Every gate whitelist inherits this (PLAN 5.6).

Genuine B-only. B's own comment concedes the V4 wording is ambiguous ('Where applicable for given commodity, user is able to filter species by type') and that it may describe UX for species filtering rather than a field. A resolves the same ambiguity the other way: no obligation, the commodity picker filters species directly. inc-002 must rule this.
Both within the line, both mandatory, both multi-select stored as an array. Pure rename.

Both within the line, both mandatory. Pure rename AS A FIELD — but A gives it a second, structural role B has no counterpart for: animalIdentifiers.maxEntriesFrom points at it, capping the nested collection at the declared animal count. See the unitRecord entry.
Same name, same depth (within the line), same gate obligation (commodityCode at the same identity level), same optional status. Whitelist membership differs (A gates on names, B on 8 codes).

Confirmed 1:1 — both notification-level, both aggregate over the lines (A frame 'anyItem', B anyAllowListed), both mandatory-or-purged. Whitelist membership differs sharply: A's CPH_COMMODITIES is ['Cow']; B's is 17 codes covering cattle, pigs, sheep, goats and the full poultry set. B's is the V4-audited list; A's predates it.

Confirmed 1:1 — both are the user-driven group nested inside the commodity line holding the per-animal identifiers, and both carry the V4 'at least one identifier' invariant (A requiredOneOf over 6 ids, B requires.anyOfIds over the same 6). Two structural keys are A-only: maxEntriesFrom (cap the collection at numberOfAnimals) and requiredAtLeastOne (a floor of 1 — B only floors commodityLine, not unitRecord). inc-002 input.

A's 'animalIdentifier' prefix is A's flat-namespace tax; B gets the same disambiguation from within: unitRecord. Both optional, both gated on the ENCLOSING line's commodity (A frame 'enclosing', B allowListed projecting onto unitRecord). Same mechanism, same semantics.
Same shape as passport. Whitelist membership differs: A ['Cat','Dog','Cow']; B ['01061900','0103','0102'] — B additionally gates in Pig.

Same shape as passport. Whitelist membership differs materially: A ['Cow']; B ['0102','0103','010410','010420'] — B gates in pigs, sheep and goats too. This one flows through to identificationDetails/description via the derived complement.
The one per-unit identifier A did NOT prefix. Both optional, both gated on the enclosing line's commodity being a horse.

Confirmed 1:1 — both are inverse gates over the union of the four typed-identifier whitelists, and both DERIVE the union rather than restating a complement. Only the operand kind differs: A's notInUnionOf takes the four sibling OBLIGATIONS and unions their activatedBy.includes; B's takes the four raw ARRAYS. Same graph, same derived union.
Shares identificationDetails' gate verbatim on both sides.
Same name, same depth (within the unit record), same gate, same mandatory-when-gated-in status. A declares it in the commodities feature; B beside the identifiers. No divergence.

inc-016b: now `within: documents` (D1 resolved). The per-record trigger — a mandatory field, no applyTo; its dependants gate on this field's per-record presence via `presentPerRecord`. Topology matches A (a document record cannot exist without a type). See the 'documents' entry.

inc-016b: `within: documents`, in scope + mandatory per record whose type is answered (presentPerRecord). PLAN 2.4: A's value is a real cdp-uploader upload; B's is a select of 8 file extensions, no bytes.
inc-016b: `within: documents`, in scope + mandatory per record whose type is answered (presentPerRecord).
inc-016b: `within: documents`, in scope + mandatory per record whose type is answered (presentPerRecord).

Genuine A-only. The pre-journey filter that decides whether the live-animals journey applies at all; it gates entry (features/import-type-filter) rather than describing consignment data, so it is outside V4's field set. B never modelled the filter step. inc-002 must rule: keep as an A-side flow concern, or admit it to the model.

D1 RESOLVED (inc-016b): B now models accompanying documents as a `documents` collection with the four fields `within` it, matching A's REPEATABLE-collection topology (no floor; V4 cap-10 enforced controller-side). Both are structural, always-in-scope, user-driven groups. Was the single biggest structural delta; documents now bridges as an ordinary A-collection <-> B-collection mapping.
Genuine A-only. The submit-time declaration tick. B has no submit at all (PLAN 2.4: zero routes, SUBMITTED is a provably dead branch), so it has nothing to declare. Stays A's per inc-016.
